name: data-validator
description: Validate data against schemas, business rules, and data quality standards.
qualityReason: Clear validation purpose with broad scope
capabilities: Validate JSON data against JSON Schema specifications
Check database DataFrame schemas with type and constraint validation
Apply custom business rules and domain-specific logic
Detect duplicates, outliers, and format issues in datasets
Generate comprehensive data quality reports with scores
Validate email, phone, URL, date, and credit card formats
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based skill containing documentation and code examples for data validation. Static findings are false positives caused by scanner misinterpreting markdown code fences as shell commands, JSON schema validation constraints as cryptographic weaknesses, and legitimate data validation patterns (email/phone/URL format checks) as reconnaissance activity. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or credential handling present.
valueStatement: Poor data quality leads to system failures and bad decisions. This skill validates data against JSON schemas, database schemas, and custom business rules to catch errors early. It also detects duplicates, outliers, and format issues while generating detailed quality reports.
userTitle: Validate data against schemas and business rules
limitations: Does not connect to databases or external data sources
Does not modify or fix data automatically
Does not execute validation on scheduled intervals
Schema validation only; does not perform data transformation

useCases.title: Validate pipeline data
useCases.description: Ensure incoming data meets schema requirements and quality standards before processing

useCases.title: Validate API contracts
useCases.description: Check request and response payloads conform to JSON Schema specifications

useCases.title: Audit data quality
useCases.description: Generate reports on completeness, uniqueness, and validity of datasets

promptTemplates.title: Basic validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate this JSON data against the provided schema. Check all required fields, data types, and format patterns. Report any validation errors.

promptTemplates.title: Business rules
promptTemplates.prompt: Check this order data against our business rules: total must match items, discount cannot exceed subtotal, shipping address required for physical items.

promptTemplates.title: Quality analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this dataset for completeness, duplicates, and format issues. Generate a quality report with scores and list the top issues found.

promptTemplates.title: Batch validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate all records in this batch against the schema. Return summary statistics: total records, valid count, invalid count, and details on each failure.

bestPractices: Define schemas and rules before validation to ensure consistency
Validate at system boundaries to catch errors early
Generate detailed error messages to help users fix issues quickly
antiPatterns: Not logging validation failures for monitoring

faq.question: What validation formats are supported?
faq.answer: JSON Schema Draft 7, Python DataFrame schemas, and custom Python functions for business rules.

faq.question: What is the maximum data size for validation?
faq.answer: Validation runs on data provided in context. Large datasets should be processed in batches.

faq.question: Can I integrate with my existing validation tools?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides patterns for jsonschema (Python), AJV (JavaScript), and pandas DataFrames.

faq.question: Is my data stored or shared?
faq.answer: No. The skill operates on data in context only. No data is stored or transmitted externally.

faq.question: Validation fails on valid data. How to troubleshoot?
faq.answer: Check schema format, ensure validators like Draft7Validator are used, and verify pattern regex syntax.

faq.question: How does this compare to built-in validation?
faq.answer: This skill provides comprehensive validation including business rules, quality scoring, and detailed reporting beyond basic type checking.
